summary:
Niraparib is an FDA-approved PARP inhibitor shown to improve progression-free survival in ovarian cancer patients, especially those with BRCA mutations or homologous recombination deficiencies, based on robust clinical trial data including the PRIMA study. Its effectiveness is most pronounced in specific molecularly defined patient groups receiving maintenance therapy after platinum-based chemo.  

(Note: The summary above includes redundant content from both rounds; please revise strictly per instructions.)

Final Summary:

Niraparib improves progression-free survival in ovarian cancer patients with BRCA mutations or homologous recombination deficiency when used as maintenance therapy after platinum-based chemotherapy. This benefit was demonstrated in major clinical trials such as PRIMA, supporting its established role in treating this population.
